changes_summary: Narrowed the revision around the real root cause: the plan now treats wrapper migration, caller z-index escape-hatch cleanup, and retirement of lightbox DOM-precedence helpers as one coordinated change. It also explicitly includes the existing `hover-card` portal wrapper, names the supporting files and tests that must move with the stack, and makes caller contract preservation versus layering-override removal concrete.

success_criteria.criterion: `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` exists, exports JSDoc-documented public actions/selectors for overlay registration, topmost queries, and stack-derived layering, and is the only automated source of truth for modal body pointer-events and focus restoration.
No raw `@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup)` imports remain anywhere under `src/` outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`.

The existing Base UI `preview-card` portal wrapper is also migrated into the centralized overlay ownership layer, or an equivalent centralized overlay wrapper path, so no in-repo Base UI portal primitive with stack-relevant z-index ownership is left outside M2.

DOM/z-index precedence helpers are retired for migrated overlay flows: `uiLayers.ts`, lightbox interaction/navigation hooks, and `LoraSelectorModal` no longer determine topmost state by querying DOM markers and comparing computed z-index values.

Caller-side layering escape hatches for migrated overlay primitives are removed or neutralized: no remaining `zIndexBase`, overlay z-index class overrides, or equivalent caller-owned numeric layering controls are needed for Dialog, Select, Popover, Menu, Tooltip, AlertDialog, or HoverCard flows.
Integration tests cover `open A -> open B -> close A -> close B` for both Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog, and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.
Tests that previously encoded the old DOM-z-index ordering strategy are updated or replaced so the suite validates the new stack-based precedence model instead.
`npm test` passes after the overlay migration.
Public wrapper entrypoints remain import-compatible for existing consumers where possible, while removing only the layering-specific escape hatches that conflict with central ownership.
The overlay stack implementation remains a small, dedicated ordered-store abstraction rather than absorbing unrelated persistence or styling responsibilities.

Deferred manual checks pass in a real app session: nested LoRA modal + Model Select closes cleanly, drag interactions do not spuriously dismiss overlays, and focus trap/Escape/restore behavior feels correct.

assumptions: The milestone should eliminate competing overlay-order mechanisms, not merely relocate raw imports, because leaving DOM-scanning precedence helpers or caller z-index overrides in place would fail the stated hardening goal.
Including `hover-card.tsx` is considered required root-cause coverage of an existing Base UI portal primitive, not unjustified scope expansion into unrelated overlay work.
`container` and non-z-index layout styling remain supported at call sites; only direct ownership of portal layering is being removed from callers.
Open order within a single shared stack is the intended replacement for current fixed above-lightbox numeric layering in nested modal flows such as LoRA over lightbox.

Automated verification remains limited to shell, typecheck, and jsdom/Vitest. Any verification that requires a live browser or manual interaction is deferred-human and should be treated as skipped if encountered during execution.
